author: yuanheng-zhao
body: Checked and matched with vendor's now. https://github.com/inclusionAI/Ming-omni-tts/blob/200a1562e33492e786c23174985bb14f8e012cc6/modeling_bailingmm.py#L452

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: We don't have any docs for these env vars imported before in previous PRs. I think we would organize and cleanup them in Q3

author: LHXuuu
body:
This is intentional and should stay aligned with the vendor implementation.

The vendor uses a 0-based `step` and stops only when:

```python
step > min_new_token
```

In vLLM-Omni we use generated-step count:

```python
(step + 1) >= min_required_decode_steps
```

So the equivalent threshold is:

```python
min_required_decode_steps = min_stop_step + 2
```

This avoids allowing the stop head one decode step earlier than HF.

author: amy-why-3459
body: self.encoder.config.num_hidden_layers is overwritten to 4 (the encoder’s actual layer count is unchanged) → any later logic that reads encoder.config.num_hidden_layers (weight loading, cache sizing, introspection) gets the wrong value;

author: amy-why-3459
body:
Is dropping explicit language/dialect intentional? (Seed-TTS EN eval likely uses Auto/en, so metrics may be unaffected, but real users setting language="粤语" lose dialect control.)
If intentional, add a test asserting the dense path does not include a dialect key when language="粤语", and document in _parse_ming_instruction / API docs that the language field is no longer forwarded on the Ming path.

author: LHXuuu
body:
This matches the vendor implementation exactly:
https://github.com/inclusionAI/Ming-omni-tts/blob/200a1562e33492e786c23174985bb14f8e012cc6/audio_tokenizer/vae_modules.py#L74-L86

The encoder module is already constructed before `config.num_hidden_layers` is changed, so the actual encoder layer count is unchanged. The mutation is only used to build the 4-layer aggregator, following the HF source.

author: LHXuuu
body:
Yes, this is intentional. For Ming-TTS, dialect control should be passed explicitly through the Ming instruction payload, e.g. `instructions='{"方言":"粤语"}'`.

The generic OpenAI `language` field is no longer forwarded as `"方言"` on the Ming path, because it caused dataset/eval language metadata such as `en` to be interpreted as a dialect instruction and diverged from the HF prompt format. Explicit `"方言"` in `instructions` is still preserved by `_parse_ming_instruction_fields`.

author: yuanheng-zhao
body: Yeah I mean this change was checked by me as well and I think it is nice 😆
